all_text: Saint Petersburg State University LYUMINGCAN Final qualifying work Protection of Sports Event Broadcasting Rights in the People`s Republic of China Level of education: master degree Field 40.04.01 "Jurisprudence" Main educational program "VM.58 43 .2021  Sports Lawyer " Scientific adviser:   Ilia Vasilyev ,   Associate Professor Reviewer:  Artur Malyshev,  Heading Counsel, RFU Saint Petersburg 2023 Catalog Abstract. 1 Introduction. 4 1. Background and significance of topic selection. 4 2. A review of research at home and abroad. 5 3. Mainly solve the problem. 7 4. Research methods. 8 1. Comparative Analysis of Sports Events and Sports Event Programs. 25 2.1.2. The object of the sports event broadcasting right is the sports event program. 28 2.2. Attributes of the object of the right to broadcast sports events. 32 2.1.1. Sports event programs should be works. 33 2.1.2. The specific category of sports events in the work. 37 3. Analysis of the current situation of the legal protection of the broadcasting rights of sports events in the People’s Republic of China. 42 3.1. The Current Situation of the Judicial Protection of Sports Event Broadcasting Rights in  the People’s Republic of China . 43 3.1.1. Judgment basis is not uniform. 43 3.1.2. Infringement damage calculation standard is not clear. 45 3.2. Reasons for Difficulty in Legal Protection of Sports Event Broadcasting Rights. 47 3.2.1. The legal basis for the protection of sports broadcast rights is vague. 47 3.2.2. Existing rights types do not cover sports broadcasting rights. 49 4. Suggestions on improving the legal protection of China's sports broadcast rights. 53 4.1. The legislative improvement of the legal protection of the broadcasting rights of sports events. 53 4.1.1. Determining the Copyright Law Protection of the Broadcasting Rights of Sports Events. 53 4.1.2. Expand the scope of protection of broadcasting rights and information network dissemination rights. 56 4.1.3. Establishment of Compensation Standards for Sports Event Broadcasting Rights Infringement. 58 4.2. The Judicial Improvement of the Legal Protection of Sports Event Broadcasting Rights. 60 4.2.1. Perfect sports intellectual property dispute trial system. 60 4.2.2. Establish a dispute resolution mechanism linking the judiciary and the administration. 62 Conclusion. 66 References. 69 Chinese Literature 69 Foreign language literature 72 Abstract . Since the beginning of the 21st century, China's comprehensive national strength has grown rapidly. With the continuous improvement of the Chinese people's spiritual and material needs for life, the sports industry has gradually entered a historical golden period of rapid and healthy development. Among them, it has obtained the right to broadcast sports events on TV .  The socio-economic benefits and income that need to be brought have always played an important role in the socio-economic value created in the field of sports in our country. But in the face of this relatively new social thing, the problems existing at the legal and institutional levels have been further exposed. China’s current relevant laws and regulations do not clearly define the nature and object of the legal definition of the right to broadcast television broadcasting of sports events. In particular, the further development and rapid popularization of the mobile Internet have brought new opportunities and challenges to the legal protection and use of sports broadcast rights in China. Sports broadcast rights infringement cases and related disputes are becoming more common, and the amount involved is also increasing. Getting bigger and bigger. Against this background, this paper discusses how to define, object, legal nature, and protection of the legal nature of sports event broadcast rights in combination with current judicial theory and practice. At present, whether the object of the right to broadcast sports events is sports events or sports events broadcast programs is the first problem that plagues scholars. The competitive sports event itself is a natural event, inherently unreformed and processed, and not directly presented in front of the eyes, so it is not the real object of the broadcasting rights of sports events. However, the images and audios of the broadcasting programs of sports events are the fruits of labo r created by relying on the material of sports events and are created by human intelligence and  labour  and then fixed through the carrier. The protection is more accurate, and at the same time, this object is original because it has undergone creative links such as processing, editing and processing, so it can meet the definition of works in China's "Copyright Law", and belongs to the specific category of compilation works. However, in judicial practice, due to the erroneous identification of the object and attributes of the broadcasting rights of sports events, and the lack of clear legal provisions, the judiciary cannot meet the basis and criteria for adjudication in the process of hearing related sports-event broadcasting rights infringement disputes. The coordination and unification have resulted in the phenomenon of different judgments in similar cases, making it difficult and costly for rights holders to protect their rights. Therefore, it is recommended to solve the problem from the following perspectives, such as speeding up the improvement of relevant laws and regulations,  clarifying the legal right of broadcasting sports events as soon as possible, expanding the existing broadcasting rights and information network broadcasting rights to improve the applicability of laws, etc. The main body of this paper consists of the following parts: the first chapter is the introduction, which briefly introduces the research background, subject content and possible innovations of this paper; the second chapter is the basic connotation of the broadcast right of sports events, mainly analyzing the concept, source and historical evolution of the broadcast right,  d emonstrate  the source and nature of the legal protection of sports event broadcast rights; the third chapter focuses on the object of sports event broadcast rights, and by comparing different theories at home and abroad, as well as the analysis of event information attributes and information property rights fit, obtain  t he proper nature of the right to broadcast sports events; the fourth chapter points out many problems and the crux of the current Chinese judicial practice on the right to broadcast sports events in light of relevant cases; the fifth chapter puts forward further Suggestions and ideas for improving the legal protection of sports broadcast rights. Keywords :  sports events; broadcast rights object; originality and irreproducibility; copyright . Introduction . Background and significance of topic selection . Throughout the development of sports broadcasting rights, it has changed from a hot yam to the most important pillar of today's event funding. At the same time, the advancement of communication technology has added to the event broadcast, and viewers can use various channels to break through time and space constraints to obtain event information consultation and event broadcast images. However, due to the lack of clear understanding of the object of this right, there are large differences in the definition of the nature of broadcasting rights in judicial practice and theoretical discussions around the world .  Communication technology has made the protection of rebroadcasting rights more challenging. The purpose of this study is: first, to provide a multi-dimensional theoretical and empirical basis for the legal confirmation of sports broadcast rights .  The framework of China's civil legal system not only provides a reasonable legal position for live broadcast rights, but also provides a feasible way for the protection of China's intangible property rights; characteristics, to give a targeted and complete originality analysis to the sports broadcast programs, and to combine the relevant provisions of the law, to point out some applicability problems existing in China's  current "Copyright Law" and the draft for review, which is the foundation of China's copyright protection system. Perfect to give advice. A review of research at home and abroad . Regarding the research on the legal attributes of sports event broadcasting rights, there are many opinions in foreign academic circles, such as the theory of stadium access represented by the Netherlands, and the theory of entertainment service provision represented by the United Kingdom,   t he theory of corporate rights represented by Italy, etc. As for the identification of the object of the sports event broadcast right, the common law countries generally use the "sweat on the forehead principle" to advocate that the object of the sports event broadcast right be recognized as work and protected by copyright. There are also many theories in the Chinese academic circles, focusing on what the object of the sports event broadcasting right is. Some scholars believe that the object of the sports event broadcasting right is the sports event itself, and according to specific characteristics, it is divided into regional formula sports events and artistic sports events to further discuss whether it constitutes a work .  Scholars represented by Professor Wang Qian believe that the object of the sports event broadcast right is the screen of  the sports event broadcast program .  Other points of view include advocating that sports event programs be divided into sports event program signals and sports event video images to further refine what is the object of sports event broadcast rights .    Secondly, regarding the legal attributes of the object of sports broadcast rights, there are also different voices in the theoretical circles. Supporters of the affirmative view, such as Professor Zhang  Houfu , believe that sports events programs fully embody artistry, intelligence, and fixedness, and should be recognized as works.    Professor Wang  Ziqiang , who disagrees, believes that sports broadcasts are still based on competitive activities, focusing on recording strength and skills, and do not have the beauty and artistic charm of literature. Therefore, they cannot be regarded as intellectual  labour  achievements and should not be placed in the copyright law category .    In addition, Professor Li  Shengwang , who holds a compromise point of view, also put forward the theory of performers' rights and believes that sports events should be specifically distinguished. Athletes and coaches in the competition can be regarded as "performers" in the competition, and they can enjoy the rights to this  labour  creation. Performer's right, only in exercising this right is required by the event  organizer .    Finally, concerning the legal protection of the rights to broadcast sports events, some judicial authorities have identified sports broadcast programs as works and protected them with copyright-related laws and regulations.    However, some judicial authorities tend to use the Anti-Unfair Competition Law as the legal basis for a trial .   Mainly solve the problem . Through a lot of analysis and research, this article mainly solves the following problems with legal thinking: first, the object of the sports event broadcasting right should be the sports event broadcasting program, not the sports competition itself; second, the sports event broadcasting program  a s  the object of the sports event broadcast right, it is the intellectual crystallization created by the relevant rights subject through labo r and technology. Its content is original and should be a work in the sense of copyright. Specifically, this article believes that the sports event broadcast program should be a compilation work. Furthermore, it can be determined that the right to broadcast sports events belongs to the category of copyright, and copyright protection for the right to broadcast sports events should be established and improved . Research methods . Based on this research background, this article attempts to study comparative law, compare relevant domestic and foreign academic views, analyze the characteristics, applicable situations and background of each view, and determine the legal attributes and rights sources of sports broadcast rights. Citing  many  judicial practice cases through empirical law, summarizing according to the phenomena in litigation activities, looking for the commonality and laws of such cases, and explaining the difficulties and challenges of the current situation of sports broadcast rights infringement cases. Overview of Broadcasting Rights for Sports Events . The premise of analyzing the legal protection of sports event broadcasting rights is to have a clear understanding of sports events and sports event broadcasting rights. With the rapid development of network media technology today .  It is necessary to re-examine and think about this issue because of  it's  specific meaning, content and attributes are all endowed by specific social practices and cannot be analyzed in isolation regardless of the actual situation, and all of these are to improve the relevant legal system. To protect the foundation of the rights subject's interests, the definition of sports events and sports event broadcast rights needs to be discussed first. Definition of broadcasting rights of sports events . Sporting event meaning . Sports events generally refer to regular events with a relatively large scale and level. Sports events in the traditional sense generally only refer to competitive sports and non-competitive activities themselves, such as the competition process between two teams within 90 minutes of regular time in football. In academia, Scholar Ling  Zongliang  believes that sports events should be carefully classified, and then consider whether they are  suitable as the object of broadcasting rights according to the classification, and then determine their legal attributes.  This point of view holds that sports events can be specifically divided into procedural sports events and artistic sports events. The former refers to sports competitions that focus on technical ability specifications. Covers sports such as football and basketball that emphasize strength, speed, and fitness for skill. The latter means that the level of athletes' competition performance no longer depends only on the degree of completion of objective rules, but the aesthetic feeling displayed in competitive activities, or the degree of combination of aesthetics and technology should be included in the judging criteria, such as gymnastics, floral art, etc. Slip and so on. Therefore, it can be described as a competitive activity that has certain aesthetic value and takes the performance of technical level and the overall artistic effect as the basis of competition results. However, with the development of social productivity and the improvement of people's living standards, the definition of "sports event" has also begun to change, and the interpretation of the word "sports event" in traditional vocabulary has lagged. The most important reason is that the content and form of sports events are constantly changing. Taking football as an example, with the addition of various new forms such as  support activities, placement advertisements, and entertainment programs .  The regular time of the minute has not changed, but the enjoyment and stimulation of multiple feelings obtained from the perspective of the audience have been greatly enriched, and a considerable part of the feelings are different from the competitive sports themselves in nature. Therefore, the current "sports event" can no longer be simply understood as a regular sports game itself. Instead, it should be seen as a collection of multiple items, multiple forms, and multiple delivery methods. The meaning of broadcasting rights of sports events . Foreign theories interpret the meaning of the right to broadcast sports events as referring to the rights enjoyed by sports organizations, event sponsors, and broadcast media to develop and utilize audio visualization of sports events, including on-site filming and reporting of events and audiovisual works formed after filming ,  disseminated in various forms. Chinese scholars believe that the right to broadcast sports events generally refers to the TV organization’s commitment to other corresponding organizations to own the TV programs of the sports events in the process of preparing or broadcasting the corresponding sports event programs. Proceeds from recording, editing or rebroadcasting.  In short, the right to broadcast sports events is the general term for the right  subject to concretize, power, and work the process of sports events. In China, the term "rebroadcasting" is not a rigorous legal term. The "Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China" refers to "rebroadcasting" mainly in two articles, respectively. Enumeration of rights in article Broadcasting Organization Rights. However, the actual meaning and specific scope of the term "rebroadcasting" are not clearly defined by laws and regulations. Therefore, the current broadcasting rights of sports events include both  re broadcasting and live broadcasting. The term "broadcasting" is more a term formed by the news industry or the sports industry in the long-term development process. The Chinese academic circles have different views on the specific concept of sports broadcast rights. The first view is that it "refers to the rights that the sponsors of sports events have for the permission of the TV reports of the games and the value brought by them".   The second point of view holds that it "refers to the right of sports organizations or event sponsors to authorize others to conduct live TV broadcasting, rebroadcasting, and recording of sports games and sports performances, and to obtain remuneration from them. The right to broadcast or disseminate to the public through media such as television, television, or the Internet".   The author believes that these three views are somewhat one-sided.   The  subject of sports event broadcast rights includes not only event organizers or sports associations but also sports media and platforms that produce signals and plan programs. The right to broadcast sports events The content is not just income and remuneration but should also cover the exclusive or even monopoly of rebroadcasting activities, that is, like paying viewers to broadcast or rebroadcast, they also have the right to exclude unauthorized third parties from transferring and using signals and images, processing and other  behaviors . Television broadcasting was an important form for people to receive sports broadcast programs at the beginning, and it may also be the longest form. Therefore, for a long period, sports broadcast rights and TV broadcast rights were confused. Considering the development of productivity at that time, this situation There is nothing wrong with it. For example, the earlier TV broadcast of the Olympic Games was first born at the Berlin Olympics in 1936, and then the London Olympics in 1948 marked the beginning of the commercialization of TV broadcast rights. The British Broadcasting Corporation obtained the broadcast of the Olympic Games for 500 pounds right. Until the 1956 Melbourne Olympic Games, television copyright was officially written into the "Olympic Charter", and the International Olympic Committee had the dominant position in this right .  This was the first international definition of the  nature and ownership of television broadcast rights for sports events. Since then, the TV broadcast income of the Olympic Games has continued to grow, but with the continuous upgrading and development of media technology, the audience's viewing methods and viewing needs have undergone tremendous changes. The traditional TV broadcast of sports events, which uses TV as the main broadcast channel, is facing severe challenges. "The openness of the Internet enables network images to be easily transmitted across national borders, which poses a challenge to the current Olympic model of separately selling TV broadcasting rights by region.   Internet companies have officially entered the big stage of Olympic commercial development, and Olympic network communication The arrival of the era has become irresistible.”   During the Beijing Olympic Games, the cooperative broadcasting of several online media and CCTV marked the official entry of sports event broadcasting into a new Internet era. Therefore, the "broadcast right" of sports events should also be understood in a broader sense. Accurately grasping the meaning of sports event broadcast rights is of great significance to the  labour  and intellectual efforts of relevant rights holders to protect their broadcast activities. Good legal protection for rebroadcasting activities. The Legal Attributes of the Right to Broadcast Sports Events . The broadcasting process of sports events is usually divided into two processes, including live broadcasting and program broadcasting. There are differences between the two in the way of expression, but because their objects are essentially a kind of specific and useful information. Especially in the context of the rapid development of media technology today, the relationship between the two has become closer and closer, gradually evolving from two categories of one thing to two stages of a process. The event organizers play a fundamental role in the live broadcast rights of the events they host. The relevant media platform's rights to the broadcast and program product of this competition are derived from this. In other words, the live broadcast right should be the root of the program broadcast right. Although the academic circle recognizes the two as different rights. But this article discusses the process of sports events in detail. Therefore, the two are summarized under the unified sports event broadcasting right to ensure the integrity of the research base on its object and related issues below. A Commentary on the Copyright Theory of Sports Event Broadcasting Rights . The actual formation time of the copyright theory of the broadcast  right of sports events can be traced back to a long time ago. The prevailing view at that time generally believed that the right attribute for producing and playing live broadcast signals and pictures was copyright.  Both the literal meaning and the actual meaning are close to the power category of copyright, but as time goes by, this statement has gradually been questioned by academic circles. The reason is that some scholars believe that sports competition itself is a natural event or activity. Although there are people involved, the "labo r" of athletes reflects their training. Although technology includes intellectual creation components, the proportion of integration into the whole game is very small. Since competitive activities are cent e red on the display of strength, skill and speed, they are far from the artistic aesthetics of the text required by the work, and the originality is far from the legal standard of the work. Therefore, the right to broadcast sports events cannot be classified as copyright in essence. In addition, there is a compromised and more flexible point of view, which believes that sports events should be further differentiated according to the difference in the proportion of influence of artistic components on the results of the competition. One is an artistic sports event in which the artistic component occupies a major or even a decisive proportion, and the other is a procedural sports event in which the technical component is the core. Then, by analyzing the  characteristics of the two types of sports events ,  we can judge the legal attributes of the rights to broadcast sports events. However, the problem that needs to be noted is that the formation of the above viewpoints is all based on a prerequisite, that is, the object of the sports event broadcasting right is the sports event itself.   This view is lagging and cannot match the actual development status of today's sports event broadcasting industry. The specific reasons will be explained in detail in the second chapter of this article in conjunction with the identification of the object and attributes of the sports event broadcast right. If the underlying foundation cannot be firmly established, the corresponding upper-level theoretical research will deviate from the direction. Therefore, the research and discussion on the sports event itself must first determine what the specific object being discussed is. It is unreasonable to conclude that the right to broadcast sports events does not belong to copyright without addressing the preconditions. Commentary on the Theory of Performer's Right in Sports Event Broadcasting Right . The emergence of this theory is complementary to the theory of copyright. The core of the theory of copyright is to judge the legal  attribute of the broadcast right of sports events through the level of originality. Products with high originality that can meet the standards of works are naturally classified into the scope of copyright, while products with low originality also need to be protected by law. Therefore, it is recognized as a "product" and protected according to the right of  neighbors . The more representative view in this theory is the performer's right theory. Scholars who hold this point of view believe that the athletes in the sports broadcast programs are participating in it. The rights to signals and images in the broadcasting of sports events are protected through performers' rights.   However, this theory is still problematic and controversial. First, this theory is consistent with the theoretical premise in the theory of copyright, that is, it still depends on the research problem of the right to broadcast sports events. They all think in advance that the object of the broadcasting rights of sports events is the sports events themselves, and directly skip the basic issues to discuss the upper-level issues. The author believes that it is more appropriate to focus on the precondition of the real object of the sports broadcast right. Secondly, to analyze this point of view, China's "Copyright Law" stipulates that others are allowed to enter and receive remuneration from live broadcasting and public transmission of their live performances. Permit others to copy, distribute and receive remuneration for audio and video recordings of  performers' performances. License others to broadcast their performances to the public via the Internet and get paid. Two problems can be found from this: first, performers' rights protect live events, and sports events certainly have many live broadcasts. However, the proportion of video replays, collection replays and even platform screenings is also increasing. The protection of performers' rights to athletes is insufficient and will tend to weaken. Second, the performer's rights stipulate that performers have the right to receive remuneration for other people's rebroadcasting, recording and other user activities. Can it be determined that the athletes have the right to charge the audience? According to reality, there is no such phenomenon. Whether it is because this part is already included in the salary of the contract signed by the athlete and the club, or how to explain that the audience pays the viewing fee to the broadcast media instead of the club. The right to broadcast sports events belongs to the performer's right, which will cause many contradictory issues. Commentary on the theory of contract creditor's right of broadcasting rights of sports events . There is a theory that the right to broadcast sports events refers to a contractual claim, that is, "the right to broadcast sports events is a contractual right arising from the constitution and the International Olympic Charter stipulates that the right to broadcast the Olympic Games  belongs to the Olympic Committee."   The reason for its judgment is that the subject of this right and the way of exercising the right are carried out through the contract.   The author thinks this view is relatively one-sided. Regarding the acquisition of rights subjects, the subject of sports event broadcast rights is not obtained through signed broadcast contracts. Instead, it declares and displays its existing subject status in the form agreed upon in the contract. That is to say, the function of the contract on the identity of the subject is not creative but a fixed function. Even if there is no contract or the contract is flawed, the subject of the sports event broadcast right can exist. Regarding the agreed method of rights exercise, rebroadcasting in a manner agreed upon by the contracting parties is indeed an important way to realize the right to broadcast sports events. However, it is too one-sided to simply think that it is the only way and then think that the right to broadcast sports events is a contractual claim. In the process of broadcasting rights for sports events, relevant media platforms sign contracts with the sponsors, and carry out broadcasting activities following the content of the agreement, which is a common form. However, it should be noted that the premise of this situation is that the media platform itself does not enjoy the main qualification for broadcasting sports events. Instead, it applies for  authorization and permission from the existing rights holders, to participate in the rebroadcasting activities to obtain profits. However, there are a large number of existing rights subjects of sports events, such as the four major sports leagues in North America, who are capable of signal production and live broadcast of the games. Therefore, based on the above two points, it is not appropriate to generalize that the right to broadcast sports events is a contractual claim. As a kind of agreed right, the contractual obligatory right has a relatively free agreement in its subject and content is an important feature and performance. However, if the subject and content of a right can be realized through agreement, it is unavoidably one-sided to determine its attribute as a contractual right. In short, to identify the attributes of sports event broadcasting rights, the key is to study their essential attributes, rather than judging by a certain form of rights realization. Viewpoint of this  graduation work. Based on the above theories, the current mainstream theories on the broadcasting rights of sports events in domestic and foreign legal circles are mainly produced in the context of broadcasting and cable television broadcasting sports events, and a unified view has not yet been formed. The author agrees with the viewpoint of copyright, that is, the right to broadcast sports events should be classified as copyright in terms of legal  attributes. This is also the keynote and foundation of this paper to study the legal protection of sports events. Before understanding the law and essence, the choice of the target must be correct. Clarifying the legal attributes of sports event broadcasting rights is based on the premise of clarifying the object of sports event broadcasting rights, and the object of sports event broadcasting rights should be sports event broadcasting programs. Therefore, the theoretical inference that the broadcasting rights of sports events are copyrights should be drawn on the premise that the broadcasting programs of sports events are recognized as works. The reasons supporting this point of view and the reasons for questioning several other points of view will be discussed and analyzed in detail below. The premise of recognizing the legal nature of the right to broadcast sports events is to recognize the objective nature of the right to broadcast sports events. Some views regard sports events as the object of sports event broadcast rights, which means that there is a deviation in the major premise of the legal attribute of sports event broadcast rights. Then the conclusions drawn deviate from reality. Especially with the popularization of the real-time broadcast of sports events on the Internet. These views are no longer applicable to the discussion of the nature of sports broadcast rights in the network environment. "The object of civil rights is the interested carrier of civil rights. In essence, civil rights are  the power of the subject to legally control the object. If there is no object, then it is meaningless to talk about civil rights."    The analysis and research on the object pointed to by the right to broadcast sports events is the premise and basis for grasping the legal attributes of the right to broadcast sports events. Therefore, clarifying the basic nature of the object of the right to broadcast sports events is the first problem to be solved. Object Identification and Attribute Analysis of Sports Event Rebroadcasting Rights . In the discussion and analysis of the broadcasting rights of sports events, it is very important to clarify the objects corresponding to the broadcasting rights of sports events. On this basis, it is necessary to identify and understand the legal attributes of the right to broadcast sports events. Then it has practical significance to better improve its legal protection. However, with the continuous changes in the social environment and the adaptive development of the sports broadcasting business itself, there are many viewpoints on the identification of the object of sports broadcasting right. This chapter aims to elaborate on and analyze the existing academic views in detail. Combined with the practical situation, explain and explain that the object of the sports event broadcasting right is the content of the sports event program rather than the sports event itself. Identification of the object of broadcasting rights of sports events . Comparative Analysis of Sports Events and Sports Event Programs . It is undeniable that sports events, that is, sports competitive activities, are the core content of sports event broadcasting activities.   It has a clear purpose and a perfect plan to determine the outcome of the game. It is a special social practice activity. In essence, sports events themselves are an objective fact. Although it is artificially held and participated, it is still unpredictable and irreproducible. It is difficult to identify the "ideological expression" and "form and carrier" itself, which is also the legal reason why it is difficult for copyright law to protect the right to broadcast sports events. If the sports event itself is the object, the first question that needs to be discussed is whether the sports event itself works. If it is affirmed that the sports event itself is a work, then the author is the athlete or the organizer of the event. If the athlete is not the author, can they be identified as a performer, and whether the athlete who is a performer has the performer's right? If it is denied that sports events themselves are works, then the right to broadcast sports events will not be protected by copyright law. If protection is carried out with the rights of  broadcasting organizers, the scope of protected subjects will be suddenly narrowed. Participating entities other than the broadcasting media TV station may not be able to obtain the protection of legitimate rights and interests. It is also in consideration of the above-mentioned difficulties that some scholars propose to partially admit and partially deny the sports events themselves. As envisaged by the classification mentioned above, "among the many sports in sports competitions, only a few can be protected by copyright law, such as rhythmic gymnastics, figure skating, synchronized swimming, etc." It is to classify hundreds of thousands of sports today. Leaving aside the influence of individual subjective factors on the identification standards of such sports events, objectively, the continuous development and updating of the content and rules of sports events is a huge problem. This is not feasible and has gone beyond the scope of using legal means to solve the problem. Therefore, the study of sports events as the object of broadcast rights will add the problem of fundamental attribute identification to the already complex object identification problem. The sports event programs are designed and arranged around sports competitions. While closely related to the sports event itself, there are also huge differences. The author believes that the sports event itself is only the material or content of the live video of the sports event. For example, facing the Great Wall, an objective historical fact in history, the  cameraman can choose different shooting angles. The light and shadow were shot with different professional lighting and composition techniques at different shooting times, and the light and shadow preserved by the technology can eventually become a fine art photography work. If a fine art painter paints its light and shadow, it can become a work of art. If the director shoots it, it may become a movie. These can all be copyrighted works of art, photography, film, or works created in a manner like filmmaking. Therefore, although these objective facts or events themselves are not the object of copyright protection, they can be the object of copyright protection after being processed and added by people, and the results of human thoughts expressed in these forms. The same is true for the relationship between the sports event itself and the screen of the sports broadcast program. In other words, the sports event program is the final form of the sports event presented to the audience, which is reflected in the TV audience after being captured by the broadcaster or the sponsor. The audience seems to watch through a lens, and what they see is not the real material itself, that is, the sports competition, but the pictures and sounds that have been professionally processed or "translated" by others. Therefore, sports events are not the natural object of the right to broadcast sports events. The object of the sports event broadcasting right is the sports event program . Sports events are indeed the highlight of sports event broadcasting activities, and the process and results of the games have always been the most concerned issues of the audience. But this situation has changed under the conditions of more colo rful programming and the design of media organizations. More and more viewers begin to watch sports games through Internet TV and various media platforms. To meet the needs of the market, the current broadcast of sports events is no longer a purely mechanical and faithful report on the trend of the game. Guest comments, interactive games, quizzes with prizes, and various advanced shooting techniques and software models began to be added to the broadcast activities. The general production process of sports event programs has been mentioned above, and here we take basketball games as an example. Relevant organizations or associations hold a basketball game, and specific media teams set up base stations around the playing field. Shoot and edit the game through portable or in-venue facilities, and then add pre-designed rehearsals or impromptu events during the game or before and after the game, and finally form a broadcast program of sports events. The particularity of sports event programs is that the program content includes both sports events and original content created by media  platforms. Sports event broadcast programs, sports events and original content are also a relationship between carrier and content. The author thinks that the sports event program is the natural object of the sports event broadcast right. First, the content of sports event programs must include the content of competitive sports events. For a broadcast program of a 90-minute football match, the competition scene of the two teams must fall into the interval of the entire broadcast program. It must be fully covered, which is also in line with the characteristics of competitive sports as the core part of the event broadcast program. Secondly, in addition to competitive sports events themselves, various activities, interactions,  performances,  and interviews actively created by media platforms have gradually become an important part of the entire game. The connection between the two is getting closer and closer, and the focus of the audience's attention is no longer all concentrated on the competition field but shifted to these creative and innovative links. If it is not possible to  analyze and evaluate the processing or even sublimation of the competition by these media platforms, it will be impossible to protect their legitimate rights and interests more clearly and efficiently comprehensively and specifically . Finally, from a practical point of view, more and more people start to watch sports games on TVs and computers, especially mobile terminals. Traditional purely competitive sports broadcasting activities have been gradually eliminated, especially with  the popularization and development of the Internet. Sports media platforms have begun to attract consumers through various sports broadcasts and broadcasts as gimmicks. The previous identification and understanding of the object of sports broadcast rights can no longer meet the requirements of the times. Respect for the needs of the audience and the fruits of the labo r of media organizations are also factors that must be taken into consideration. Therefore, compared with sports events themselves, sports events programs with rich content and various forms are more suitable as the object of sports event broadcast rights, which is also reflected in China's judicial practice. Taking the case of Sports Olympic Power v. Tudou.com as an example, the court held that sports events and sports programs are completely different, and sports events are an objective fact.  Without pre-design and rehearsal, the natural result of the game cannot be predicted in advance. On the other hand, sports event broadcast images or videos are shot under the control of the human subjective will, adding elements such as commentary, subtitles, interviews, playback shots or close-ups, which are fixed on a specific carrier. When it is fixed on a specific carrier, it will be deterministic, unique and reproducible. To sum up, the sports event itself is an objective legal fact. There is neither the expression of thought nor the final form in which it emerges.  Therefore, it should not be used as the object of the broadcasting rights of sports events. However, after sports events are processed and recorded by the event organizers, fixed sports event programs are more suitable as the object of sports event broadcast rights. This kind of identification has many advantages. First, it is helpful to explain the legal relationship of different subjects in the broadcasting rights of sports events. For example, live audiences enter the venue to enjoy sports events through tickets, while TV stations or media use a lot of funds and manpower to carefully broadcast or broadcast the games. At the same time, many viewers watched the screen on personal terminals through channels such as the Internet. If it is considered at this time that the objects of the broadcast activities accepted by the three subjects are pure sports events themselves, it will be difficult to explain the huge difference in the expenses paid by the three subjects. If the sports event and the screen of the sports event broadcast program are distinguished, the above problems can be classified and discussed to solve the problem. Second, the broadcasting of sports events is an artificial process of presenting an objective fact in front of the audience, and the sports events themselves are different from natural scenery and handicrafts. It does not have a strict sense of carrier and expression of thought. The images of sports events can be recorded and disseminated by TV signals, network data, etc. Compared with the sports events themselves, the broadcast programs of sports events are  more savable and fixed, and it is easier to locate them when it comes to legal protection so that they can be better protected. Third, if the sports event itself is used as the object, it is easy to cause many derivative problems. For example, the question of value judgment with vague standards such as reproducibility and originality, and whether the sports event itself constitutes a work involves related rights and interests of authors and performers. Attributes of the object of the right to broadcast sports events . In the previous chapter, through analysis and discussion, we determined that "sports event programs" as a whole should be the object of sports event broadcast rights. Further analysis of its legal nature is still required. At present, there have been a large number of disputes related to the broadcasting rights of sports events in judicial practice, but the legal basis and judgment results of judicial organs are quite different. The root cause is that the judiciary's understanding and determination of the object attributes of sports broadcast rights are not clear and uniform. As mentioned in the above article, sports event programs have undergone tremendous changes with the advancement of related technologies. Both content and form have become larger and more complex, which has indeed brought greater difficulties to the confirmation of its legal  attributes. In a summary, it discusses the attributes of the object of broadcasting rights for sports events. It is to discuss the degree of originality of sports events programs. In other words, discuss whether it can reach the height of a work protected by copyright, or how to determine its nature when it cannot meet this requirement. Sports event programs should be works . At present, the current Chinese law regards originality, reproducibility, and legality as the criteria to measure whether the work meets the requirements. From the point of view of this article, there is no need to specifically discuss replicability and legality. It is necessary to comment on whether the object of the rebroadcast right is real work. The core issue is to evaluate the degree of originality of the object of broadcasting rights of sports events. Scholars who "oppose works" represented by Professor Wang Qian in Chinese academic circles believe that "the originality embodied in the TV programs of the competition is not enough to constitute works created by methods similar to film production as stipulated in China's copyright law."  It is more reasonable to consider the live broadcast images that reflect the process of the sports game itself as "recorded". Th i s point of view  believe  that the programs of sports events themselves have certain technical specifications and rules to  follow. If multiple directors have reached a certain level of technology, the gap will not be too significant when faced with the same screenshot from different angles for real-time selection.   However, the author believes that the basis for this view may not be reasonable in today's fast-changing broadcast technology, especially the Internet environment. Sports event programs are different from sports events themselves. As sports events are competitive activities of athletes, they are not reproducible. In the academic field, there is a consensus that sports events do not constitute works and are not protected by copyright law. But different from sports events, sports events programs are a specific media platform. Use specific equipment and methods to record or broadcast the game. At the same time or with a slight delay, the program picture and sound are edited and perfected. This shows that in terms of appearance alone, the sports event program and the external manifestation of the sports event are not completely consistent. A sporting event is a relationship contained in a sporting event program. There are two specific differences in content. First, it cannot be ignored that the various projects, designs, and shots involved in the broadcast programs are based on sports events. Although the external performance is improvised and random to a certain extent there are advanced designs and ideas behind these projects. Not  exactly an emergency strategy for field workers. The instant response of the director of the sports event program to the objective situation of the game scene is based on a lot of preparation, thinking and rehearsal in advance, not a random, impromptu temporary decision. This process is highly like the oral works in the works. Oral works include two processes, one is the design, arrangement, deduction and other links before the performance, and the other is the impromptu response during the performance, the performance following the trend, and such impromptu lectures, court debates, etc. Works expressed in oral language have been included in the protection category of works. As sports events with more detailed and tighter pre-arrangement and design, programs should also be included in the category of works. Secondly, although these projects, designs, and shots have been designed and arranged in advance and are carried out around sports events. But what cannot be ignored is that it still contains a lot of divergence and innovation. It is undeniable that both the program design and arrangement personnel and the site interview and filming personnel may have received similar training, use the same equipment, and may be relatively similar in basic business capabilities and methods. However, it is also based on the unpredictability of sports events that even at the same time, at the same place, different technicians react differently under the same situation and thus different programs appear. For example, in a  basketball game, for wonderful teamwork, different programs on different media platforms may adopt completely different editing strategies and may insert different links such as technical analysis, camera playback, close-up shooting, and character interviews. Eventually, several similar segments and games were combined into a complete sports broadcast. Even if multiple staff members made the same shooting or arrangement, its originality cannot be denied. For example, multiple photographers may take pictures of a flying bird at the same time, and the result is that the photos of multiple people have a high degree of overlap. But as we all know, in this case, each photographer still owns the copyright to his photos and will not consider these photos not to belong to the category of works because similar angles lack originality. In judicial practice, the "Sina v.  Tianying   Jiuzhou  Case" also identified the live broadcast of sports events as a work protected by the Copyright Law for the first time. Considering that it has sufficient originality, the court held that:  O riginality means independent creation without imitation or plagiarism of other people's works. The broadcast production of the event is based on the setting of uncertain recording equipment for shooting and recording. The above-mentioned pictures  The  formation is the result of the director's selection of shots. In this process, different camera settings, different screen selections, arrangements, cutting and other multiple means will present different event images,  which should be considered as the selection and arrangement of event recording shots. Undoubtedly, it is a kind of creative labo r, and the creativity will produce different pictures from different choices and different productions, which just reflects its originality, which also affirms the degree of originality of sports events programs on the other hand.   The specific category of sports events in the work . Compared with Professor Wang Qian's theory of "opposing works", the other side holds the theory of "supporting works". For example, Professor Cong  Lixian  believes that "according to the provisions of China's current copyright law, the overall live broadcast of sports events can constitute a compilation work."  Lawyer Rong Chao believes that "live sports events are works in the sense of copyright law. It is a work created in a way like filming".   The author believes that sports event programs do not belong to similar film works, but to compilation works. Film-like works are fully described as "works produced like filmmaking". Item 11 of Article 4 of the "Regulations for the Implementation of the Copyright Law" stipulates that film works and  works produced in a manner like a film production refer to filming on a certain medium. It consists of a series of pictures with or without sound and is shown with the help of appropriate devices or disseminated in other ways. If sports events are identified as similar to film works, the following situations will occur. After comparison, it can be seen that similar to film works There are still fundamental differences between sports events and sports events. The two are relatively similar in terms of shooting methods, storage media and transmission channels. However, similar movies usually have a shooting script as the basis, while the "script" of sports events programs is  a   sports competition. On the one hand, the script itself of a film-like work is a work in the strict sense of originality, reproducibility, and legality. However, the "script" of a sports event program, that is, the sports event itself is not a work, but a general objective fact. Therefore, in terms of constituent elements, the two are not the same. On the other hand, personnel in the production process of quasi-movie works and sports events programs may play their subjective initiative to carry out secondary creation. But, the creation of similar movies can change the design of the original script to a certain extent, such as deleting important plots, inserting new characters and new props, etc. However, in the process of enriching the program content around sports events, no matter what form It is impossible for the design and arrangement to directly affect the sports event itself. For example, when  comparing the data of the core players of the two teams, different guests, different equipment, and different lenses may be used to evaluate the progress of the game. But in the end, it is impossible to directly change the data of the players and performance and will not affect the trend of the game. In addition, similar film works are the same as film works, emphasizing the integrity of the work, often commonly known as "a team", while the content of sports events is just the opposite. It is an aggregate formed by the combination of various relatively independent programs. Generally, it is a combination of independent creations by several rights holders. Sports events programs are not suitable to be identified as the like film works. Compilation works refer to Article 14 of the "Copyright Law": "Compilation of several works, fragments of works or data or other materials that do not constitute works and works that reflect originality in the selection or arrangement of their contents are called compilation works." The components of a compilation work can be works and fragments protected by copyright law, such as essays, entries, poems, pictures, etc. It can also be data or other materials that are not protected by copyright laws, such as laws and regulations, stock market information, commodity quotations, etc. As mentioned above, sports event programs are recorded by the media platform through many personnel and equipment, self-arranged and planned from multiple angles and aspects  around sports events, and creative design, arrangement, editing, processing, etc. are carried out by giving full play to the subjective initiative. Made from multiple links. The provisions of Article 14 of China's "Copyright Law" on compilation works protect this kind of creative arrangement. Such a determination is also very beneficial in protecting the civil rights of various rights subjects, such as commentators' evaluation and prediction of the game trend. In terms of copyright legal relationship, it is very likely to form an independent oral work or a director's highlights of the competition, and it is also very likely to form a film-like work or even a film work alone. If the sporting event program is still defined as a film work or a work created by a method similar to film production at this time, according to the provisions of the Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China, the copyright of the work is enjoyed by the producer, and the director, director, director, Lyricist, composer, photography, etc. Authors have the right to sign. In this case, if a third party infringes the copyright of an oral work or other works in the overall program, the author of the work cannot fully protect the legal rights of the work. To sum up, although the original theory on the nature of the object of the right to broadcast sports events has a certain basis at the beginning of its appearance. However, in the reality of the rise of mobile terminals and emerging Internet media today, the identification of the attributes of  sports events programs should keep pace with the times and adapt to the trend. The author believes that it is more reasonable to define the object of broadcast rights of sports events as compilation works. Analysis of the current situation of the legal protection of the broadcasting rights of sports events in  the People ’ s Republic of China . With economic development and social progress, people's demand for spiritual and cultural life continues to grow. In this context, the people's government and relevant associations are actively promoting the transformation of the structure of sports events, and market capitalization has also seized the opportunity to actively pour into this field. The types, quantity, and scale of sports events have undergone tremendous changes. The Internet has promoted the vigorous growth of the broadcasting of sports events. However, many problems have been exposed in the process of rapid growth, such as pirate recording and broadcasting through technical means. The following will analyze this problem through relevant cases and examples. The Current Situation of the Judicial Protection of Sports Event Broadcasting Rights in  the People ’ s Republic of China . Judgment basis is not uniform . The right to broadcast sports events is still not a statutory right in my country. At present, my country has not passed clear legislation to define the relevant concepts of broadcast rights activities of sports events. Therefore, it is reflected in actual judicial activities that different courts still have great differences in judgments even for the same type of sports event broadcast infringement. In the 2010 case of "CCTV International Network Company v. Century Dragon Information Network Company", the court held that the focus of the dispute was the lack of originality of the football match rebroadcast program, which did not meet the requirements of works in the sense of copyright law, so it did not constitute a film work or similar .  The works created by filming were only recognized as audio and video products, and the final judgment only supported part of the plaintiff’s claims .   Similar to it is the 2015 "CCTV International Litigation Storm Group World Cup Case"   a nd "CCTV  International v.  Huaxia  City  Television  Network Company" .  However, in the 2015 "Sina.com v. Beijing  Tianying   Jiuzhou  Network Technology Co., Ltd. Chinese Super League Dispute over Copyright Infringement and Unfair Competition", the court held that there was no statutory standard for the originality of the work, and the program producer had no legal basis for editing and arranging the footage.  Interlude and interleaving are also a kind of creative labo r, so the sports event broadcast program has originality in the sense of copyright law and can be deemed as a work, but because the originality is relatively limited, it is finally determined to be a work created by a method similar to film production, the court ruled following the law to support the plaintiff SINA's claim. Secondly, on the premise of constituting a work, there is also controversy over what terms should be used to protect the online broadcast activities that have become mainstream media platforms. For example, in the above-mentioned "Sina v.  Tianying   Jiuzhou  case", the court relied on the Copyright Law of Article 10, paragraph 1, item 17 is a catch-all clause, affirming that this rebroadcasting right belongs to "other rights that should be enjoyed by the copyright owner". The difference is that in the "   CCTV International Network Company v. Century Dragon Information Network Company   ", the court's ruling was based on Article 10, Paragraph 1, Item 12 of the "Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China", which protected its  rebroadcasting activities based on the right of information network dissemination. The court hearing the case of "CCTV International v.  Huaxia  City Television Network Co., Ltd." based its judgment on Articles 2 and 20 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China. Infringement damage calculation standard is not clear . For general sports event broadcast rights disputes, the issue of damages must be involved after the defendant is determined to be infringing. The non-uniform calculation of damage compensation standards in such cases has resulted in a large discrepancy between the amount of compensation calculated by the plaintiff and the amount of compensation finally awarded by the court in many sports event broadcast rights infringement cases. The compensation factors are also different. This phenomenon will undoubtedly increase the difficulty and cost of rights protection for rights holders. Now select the sports event broadcast rights infringement cases that have received more attention in recent years to illustrate the impact of the determination of the nature of the sports event broadcast program on the infringement judgment and the number of damages. The details are as follows (see Table 1 ). The impact of the determination of the nature of sports broadcast programs on damages (Table 1) Case Name Identification of nature Protection basis Compensation claims/judgments (10,000 yuan) Compensation ratio (%) Sina v.  Tianying  Kyushu Work Copyright 1000/50 5 CCTV International v. Beijing  Fengxing Work Copyright 400/10 2.5 CCTV International v. Century Dragon Work Neighbouring  rights 210/20 9.5 CCTV International v. Beijing  Baofeng  (first instance) Work Neighbouring  rights 400/67.24 16.81 CCTV International v. Beijing  Baofeng  (Second Instance) Work Neighbouring  rights 400/400 100 CCTV International v. Beijing I love chat network company Non-work Unfair competition 100/40 40 It is not difficult to find out through the existing representative cases of infringement of broadcasting rights of sports events. First, the determination of the nature of broadcasting programs of sports events is directly related to the judicial basis for its legal protection. Secondly, even if a determination of the same nature is adopted However, there is still a lack of clear damage compensation standards and calculation methods, resulting in large differences in the determination of the specific  amount of compensation in similar cases. As far as rights and interests are concerned, it will undoubtedly greatly increase the cost of rights protection. Reasons for Difficulty in Legal Protection of Sports Event Broadcasting Rights . As mentioned above, sports events, a booming emerging industry, cannot progress and grow better without theoretically supported rights protection at the legal level. However, the current actual situation in China is that there is a lack of clear legal provisions at the legislative level . ,  in judicial practice, it is impossible to achieve a unified judgment and effective regulation of relevant infringements. The author believes that the main reason for the phenomenon is still the lack of clarity on the legal nature of the broadcast right of sports events and the object of the broadcast. The following will continue to explain  The  author's thinking on the legal protection dilemma of China's current sports broadcast rights. The legal basis for the protection of sports broadcast rights is vague . Understanding the right to broadcast sports events can be divided into two levels: "sports" and "broadcast". First of all, from the perspective of sports, the most important piece of legislation in the field of  professional sports in China is the "Sports Law of the People's Republic of China", but the legal provisions focus on sports itself, that is, competitive sports in a narrow sense, and do not involve television. The broadcasting of sports events by the media and the Internet naturally does not have much theoretical support for judicial practice. Secondly, from the perspective of broadcasting, the "Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China" does not have a legal definition of the right to broadcast sports events, that is, it does not recognize the right to broadcast sports events as a legal right, while the "Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China" "It also simply emphasizes the rules of fair competition and the purchase of specific commodities in Articles 2 and 6, and there are no specific regulations on the rights to broadcast sports events. It can be seen that the definition, scope, content, and infringement compensation related to the right to broadcast sports events have no clear answers in my country's current legal system. Principles are used as the basis, which greatly increases the ambiguity of the basis for the judgment. The essence of the fuzzy legal basis is still the lack of understanding and unclear identification of the legal nature of the right to broadcast sports events and its objective issues. Through relevant judicial practice, it can be found that although most judicial agencies have gradually tended to regard sports broadcasts as works However, there is still disagreement on the issue of specific  categories. Some courts have ruled that broadcasting of sports events belongs to audio and video recordings in their judgments.  Another court held that the broadcasting of sports events is a work created in a similar way to filming, and the reason for this disagreement is that there is currently no clear legal provision in China to define the object and attributes of the right to broadcast sports events.  There are differences in understanding of the legal attributes of the broadcasting rights of sports events, which leads to differences in referees. Existing rights types do not cover sports broadcasting rights . Since the beginning of the 2008 Beijing Olympic Games, the broadcasting technology of TV stations has continued to improve, and the broadcasting activities of sports events have a higher level and a wider scope. It is not difficult to find the relevant legal basis to standardize phenomena such as chains in China's existing legal system, but now the technical means of broadcasting are constantly diversifying, especially since the advent of the Internet era has completely changed the broadcasting of sports events. For example, Tencent and the alliance have reached an exclusive broadcast contract worth 1.5 billion for 5 years. Another example is that e-sports given to the network platform has  become an internationally officially recognized sports competition and is developing rapidly. This aspect shows that the field of sports broadcast  On  the other hand, it also shows that the broadcasting activities of many sports events may all enter the digital age of the Internet. However, the types of rights in China's current legal system have not fully covered the rights to broadcast sports events. First of all, although China's "Copyright Law" has added information network broadcasting rights on time, the current main adjustment objects of this right are still limited to interactive network transmission methods, so that "the public can obtain works at the time and place selected by individuals. Performances or audio and video recording rights", and the current sports event broadcasting programs are still classified as non-interactive due to the various limitations of the core sports event itself and technical bottlenecks, so it is difficult to be covered by the network information broadcasting rights, is well protected. Secondly, in this case, whether the right to broadcast can explain the rebroadcasting of sports events on the Internet has also become a question worth considering. The rebroadcasting right in China's "Copyright Law" is stipulated as "broadcasting right, that is, public broadcasting or dissemination by wireless means  w orks , the right to communicate broadcast works to the public employing cable transmission or rebroadcasting, and the right to communicate broadcast works to the public through loudspeakers or other similar tools for transmitting  symbols, sounds, and audio-visual images", its connotations can be summarized into three types, That is, over-the-air broadcasting, cable relay broadcasting, and public broadcast broadcasting. Considering that the connotations of wireless broadcasting and public broadcasting are far from the characteristics of sports event broadcasting rights, whether cable broadcasting covers sports event broadcasting rights has become controversial,  the convention holds that cable retransmission must be a rebroadcast of a work that has already been broadcast in a wired manner and the object must be a wireless broadcast work ，  It also means that "cable" does not include "network", and judging from the current trend that the broadcasting rights of sports events are gradually transferred to the Internet, this requirement is not in line with the broadcasting activities of sports events.  The right to broadcast also fails to fully cover and protect the right to rebroadcast sports events. From the previous explanations, we can know that there are still many difficulties and challenges in the legal protection of sports broadcasting activities in China. The fundamental reasons are not only subjective disputes over the object and attributes of sports broadcasting rights but also objective legislation. There is a certain lag. Suggestions on improving the legal protection of China's sports broadcast rights . The root cause of the insufficient protection of sports broadcast rights in China's current judicial practice is that there are gaps in the legislative and judicial fields. According to learn from typical cases, summarize the rules, and provide ideas and references for the judiciary to deal with existing sports event broadcast rights disputes through judicial interpretations and guiding cases; in addition, redefine or explain the meaning of existing legal terms It is also one of the effective measures to make its adjustment scope adapt to the current actual needs, and to fully incorporate the rights of broadcasting sports events into legal protection. The legislative improvement of the legal protection of the broadcasting rights of sports events . Determining the Copyright Law Protection of the Broadcasting Rights of Sports Events . Legislative protection is the basic work to protect a right. To protect the rapidly changing and developing sports broadcasting activities, it is necessary to clarify the legal basis for the legal protection of sports broadcasting rights as soon as possible. At present, some scholars believe  that the broadcasting rights of sports events should be adjusted and protected by the existing "Sports Law of the People's Republic of China". interests, and then at the least sacrifice of other interests” . Sports events are activities created by investors investing a lot of money, and the "Copyright Law" mainly focuses on encouraging intellectual creation rather than capital investment. Only by including the rights to broadcast sports events in the adjustment scope of the "Sports Law" can the rights to broadcast sports events be highlighted as property attributes. The author has different opinions on this point. First, from the perspective of necessity, the development speed of sports event broadcasting in recent years has far exceeded that of the past ten years. The technology and means of broadcasting have undergone tremendous changes, fundamentally changing .  The basic content and form of broadcasting programs or activities of sporting events, regardless of the sports itself, or the broadcasting activities of this sport, is certainly the result of a large amount of capital investment, but the intellectual creation factor of this overall activity cannot be denied.  T he comparison of capital investment and intellectual creation is targeted, and property attributes cannot be recognized in isolation on the grounds of the huge amount of funds. Secondly, from the perspective of possibility, from the current legislative situation, the "Sports Law" is mainly a matter of adjusting and regulating  sports competitions in the administrative field. Although the target object is the sports event broadcast program or sensory images as mentioned in the previous chapters, the main body of its intellectual creation and labo r is the director, photographer, etc. rather than the athletes. This is already a consensus in the academic circles. It is not appropriate to include the right to broadcast events in the Sports Law for protection. On this issue, the author supports the legal protection of the right to broadcast sports events by improving the "Copyright Law". Specific measures should be to clarify the fundamental issue of the legal nature of the sports event broadcast right through legislation. As mentioned above, the object of the sports event broadcast right should be the sports event broadcast program, and the degree of originality can reach the level of copyright law. The standard of works should be included in the classification of compiled works in the Copyright Law for protection. The legal nature of the right to broadcast sports events should fundamentally belong to copyright, and clarifying this issue through legislation is conducive to the formation of a unified and specific legal basis in practice so that the subjects of rights involved in the broadcast of sports events have rules to follow, to more accurately evaluate and guide its legal actions, so that judicial and administrative agencies have laws to follow, efficiently and coordinately resolve disputes over infringement of live broadcasting rights of sports events, and protect the legitimate rights and  interests of  obliges . Expand the scope of protection of broadcasting rights and information network dissemination rights . As mentioned in the previous chapter of the article, the legal protection of sports event broadcast rights through "Copyright Law" is a more desirable method, but the existing "Copyright Law" still lags and deserves to be improved. Especially in the face of the current trend that networked broadcasting has gradually become the mainstream method of broadcasting sports events, it is possible to cover sports events broadcasting rights by expanding the interpretation of the existing broadcasting rights and information network dissemination rights .  China's broadcasting rights are subject to statutory adjustments stipulated in the "Copyright Law" as wireless broadcasting, cable broadcasting and public broadcasting. As mentioned above, the "wired broadcasting" here does not include the Internet, whether it is interpreted literally or from the original intention of the legislation, this setting was to adapt to the information dissemination method at that time, that is, the wireless transmission method under the mechanical copying method, but the popularity of the Internet information interaction method represented by P2P transmission has greatly changed the original information acquisition  method of the broadcasting right. The author believes that today when network communication has gradually become the mainstream method, it is not feasible from a technical point of view to determine whether the communication method and source signal form of the infringing object are wired or wireless in the face of infringement cases. Therefore, the solution to this dilemma is to include "Internet broadcasting" in "cable broadcasting", or delete the "cable" restriction in "cable broadcasting", so that the broadcasting of sports events through the Internet can be  p rotected under the provisions of the broadcasting right. As for the information network broadcasting right, it can also be seen from the above that with the current legal definition and interpretation, it is difficult for the sports event broadcasting right to be protected by the information network broadcasting right. Due to the maturity of Internet technology, that is, interactive experiences such as real-time review and free access have gradually become the attributes of the entire Internet. Whether sports events broadcast programs are interactive requires new considerations; on the other hand, Internet communication is the main mode of communication. The information began to cover more and more non-interactive information if it is no longer technically possible and legally necessary to insist on the transmission channel and signal source nature of the information network rebroadcast right. Therefore, the right of information network dissemination should no longer be constrained by  the definition of interactive methods, but the new feature of non-interactive transmission can be added, or the rigid requirement of interactive transmission can be deleted, so that the right to rebroadcast through information networks can be established. Pave the way for the protection of sports broadcasts in the online domain. Establishment of Compensation Standards for Sports Event Broadcasting Rights Infringement . The commercial value contained in sports broadcasting activities is increasing. At the same time, infringements such as piracy and hotlinking of sports broadcasting rights have also formed a chain of interests with industries such as webcasting and sports betting. Compared with the obtained  In  terms of benefits, lower asset costs and  labour  costs make such infringements emerge endlessly. Therefore, a scientific and reasonable sports event broadcast rights infringement compensation mechanism has become a key measure to curb related infringements. Establish a compensation standard for sports event broadcast rights infringement, should be carried out from several aspects .
